headline: Diebold Nixdorf Set to Join S&P SmallCap 600
summary: Diebold Nixdorf Inc. (NYSE: DBD) will replace Sealed Air Corp. (NYSE: SEE) in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective prior to the opening of trading on Friday, April 10. Clayton, Dubilier & Rice LLC is acquiring Sealed Air in a deal expected to be completed April 9.

headline: Shareholder Proposals Test S&P Global Governance As Valuation Looks Stretched
summary: Shareholders at S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) have filed proposals ahead of the May 2026 annual meeting. Requests include greater transparency around charitable contributions and easier terms for shareholders to call special meetings. The company has recommended voting against these proposals, putting governance and shareholder rights in focus. S&P Global, known for its credit ratings, indices, and financial data services, sits at the center of information flows that many investors and...

headline: Have markets finally bottomed amid latest in US-Iran saga?
summary: Fidelity Investments director of global macro Jurrien Timmer joins Julie Hyman on Market Catalysts to share his analysis of how markets (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are reacting to the ongoing Iran war.

headline: Lebenthal: NVDA and Micron account for nearly all S&P tech earnings growth
summary: Here is a number worth sitting with: strip Nvidia and Micron out of Q1 S&P earnings and overall S&P earnings growth drops to roughly 3%. That came from James Lebenthal in a recent interview, and I haven’t stopped thinking about it since. Tech is expected to grow earnings roughly 30% this year, and virtually all ... Lebenthal: NVDA and Micron account for nearly all S&P tech earnings growth

headline: Barclays analysts see "contained" volatility as Iran ceasefire deadline approaches
summary: Investing.com -- Barclays analysts said Tuesday that equity markets expect a contained outcome, even with the Iran war ceasefire deadline approaching. President Trump made clear that another ceasefire extension is highly unlikely.

raw.headline: Barclays analysts see "contained" volatility as Iran ceasefire deadline approaches
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Barclays analysts said Tuesday that equity markets expect a contained outcome, even with the Iran war ceasefire deadline approaching. President Trump made clear that another ceasefire extension is highly unlikely.

headline: CARFAX Report Now Delivers Future Reliability Based on a Vehicle's Unique VIN-Specific History
summary: For the first time, consumers can compare the future Reliability of specific cars they might consider buying, which will help them confidently make the right choice for their next vehicle purchase. Until now, reliability information from other companies has been based on general information, such as a vehicle's make and model. Only CARFAX can provide the future Reliability for a specific car, never before seen in the industry, giving shoppers the long-term perspective they need to choose the rig

headline: Could Gartner Stock's Cash Flow Spark the Next Rally?
summary: We think Gartner (IT) stock is worth a look: It is growing, producing cash, and available at a significant valuation discount. Companies like this can use cash to fuel additional revenue growth, or simply pay their shareholders through dividends or buybacks. Either move makes them attractive to the market.

headline: A Look At S&P Global (SPGI) Valuation After Services PMI Signals First Contraction In Over Three Years
summary: S&P Global (SPGI) shares are back in focus after its latest services purchasing managers index slipped to 49.8 in March, the first sub-50 reading in more than three years. See our latest analysis for S&P Global. The weaker services PMI has landed against a mixed backdrop for the stock, with a 7 day share price return of 3.96% but a 90 day share price return showing a 19.56% decline. The 3 year total shareholder return of 31.29% indicates that longer term holders have still seen gains, so...
